headlines.title: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation
headlines.description: Sir Keir Starmer is planning a law which will mean that the UK government can adopt EU single market rules, without them being voted on in Parliament.

headlines.title: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
headlines.description: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

headlines.title: Katya Adler: Celebration in Budapest, relief in Brussels but a blow for Moscow
headlines.description: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

headlines.title: The surprising reality of how teenage girls still define themselves
headlines.description: Despite strides in feminism, why do teen girls still seem to view themselves through the lens of boys?

headlines.title: Deep-fried food and daily sausage rolls banned in new school dinner plans
headlines.description: Schools are being told to cut down on sugary desserts, and provide more vegetables and whole grains.

headlines.title: Rachel Zegler's heroes, prizes for Paddington and more moments from the Olivier Awards
headlines.description: The 50th Oliviers at the Royal Albert Hall provided some memorable conversations away from the ceremony.

headlines.title: PM to scrap spy chiefs' Hillsborough Law veto
headlines.description: Security chiefs will not be able to block spies from giving evidence to future public inquiries.

headlines.title: Trump attacks Pope over criticism of Iran war
headlines.description: The US president accuses Leo of being "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy".

headlines.title: Swinney says Scottish independence referendum could be held in 2028
headlines.description: The SNP leader made the claim in the BBC's televised debate ahead of next month's Scottish election.

headlines.title: Britney Spears goes into rehab after driving under the influence arrest
headlines.description: The singer voluntarily checks herself into a treatment facility, three weeks before her court date.

headlines.title: UK will not join blockade of Iran's ports in the Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: UK minesweepers and anti-drone capabilities will continue operating in the region, as pressure ramps up to reopen the key shipping route.

headlines.title: Trump's blockade threat raises risks and leaves predicaments unchanged
headlines.description: The conflict is now a test of wills - Iran’s capacity to absorb strikes versus Trump’s tolerance for the war's costs.

headlines.title: What is a naval blockade and how would it work?
headlines.description: Donald Trump says that the US is going to start blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

headlines.title: Twenty-one hours of back and forth leave US and Iran far apart
headlines.description: US Vice-President JD Vance was a difficult mission, negotiating on behalf of a president whose messaging on the war has been mixed.

headlines.title: Oil prices rise back above $100 after peace talks fail
headlines.description: The failure of negotiations at the weekend has raised concerns that the global energy crisis will deepen.

headlines.title: Rory McIlroy defends Masters title, underlining why he is one of golf's greats
headlines.description: Moments after winning the Masters last year, Rory McIlroy said it would be transformative. He has proved that by defending the title at Augusta National.

headlines.title: Tom Hiddleston, Cate Blanchett and Paddington walk Oliviers red carpet
headlines.description: Stage stars posed for pictures ahead of a night of celebration for UK theatre at the Royal Albert Hall.

headlines.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
headlines.description: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule,  Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'PM refuses to join blockade' and 'Hungary enters new era'
headlines.description: Starmer ruling out the UK's role in the US blockade of Iran's ports and Hungary electing a new leader lead Monday's papers.

headlines.title: The Dutch village at risk of being demolished
headlines.description: Moerdijk has been earmarked for removal, to make way for a vast electricity substation.

headlines.title: On the road with Canadian forces training for a new Arctic reality
headlines.description: Across 5,200 km of Canada's Arctic, some 1,300 members of Canada's military spent two winter months on a mission to meet the challenges of a changing north.

headlines.title: The BBC Scotland election debate fact-checked
headlines.description: Claims made by leaders of six political parties who took part in a TV debate for the 2026 Holyrood election campaign are checked.

headlines.title: Wider licence extensions for pubs during World Cup
headlines.description: The government had previously announced opening hour extensions in England and Wales for some knockout matches - but has now widened the eligibility.

headlines.title: Work begins in Wales on UK's first small modular reactor nuclear power station
headlines.description: Rolls-Royce has said the project will create 8,000 jobs across Britain.

headlines.title: Eight to be awarded for bravery in Bondi Junction stabbing in Sydney
headlines.description: Six people were killed and ten injured after a man went on a stabbing rampage in an Australian mall.

headlines.title: Cold sore virus left mum with brain damage
headlines.description: Helen Edwards initially thought she had flu, but then spent 12 weeks in hospital and was diagnosed with encephalitis.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Artemis II: Return to the Moon
headlines.description: Four astronauts. One historic mission. Venturing further from Earth than ever before.

headlines.title: US-Iran Talks Fail - What Happens Next?
headlines.description: And what stopped an agreement?

headlines.title: Unpack all the latest drama from Race Across the World
headlines.description: Celebrity superfans and travel expert Alfie Watts join Tyler West to discuss the episode!

headlines.title: How young men's lives are influenced by the manosphere
headlines.description: James Blake enters the manosphere, joining men seeking status, wealth and relationships.

headlines.title: Where does McIlroy rank among greats after Masters defence?
headlines.description: Rory McIlroy has added a rare Masters defence to the career Grand Slam - now he is aiming to keep climbing the all-time list of major victories.

headlines.title: McIlroy holds nerve to win second successive Masters
headlines.description: Rory McIlroy holds his nerve to shoot a one-under par final round to win the 2026 Masters at Augusta, one shot ahead of Scottie Scheffler.

headlines.title: The Masters that got away - Rose the nearly man again
headlines.description: Justin Rose says his latest run at the Masters was the "chance that got away" after falling short at Augusta once more.

headlines.title: Man City have psychological edge over Arsenal - Rooney
headlines.description: Wayne Rooney says Manchester City have the psychological edge over leaders Arsenal in the Premier League title race.

headlines.title: Will Romero's tears become the enduring image of Tottenham's season?
headlines.description: Cristian Romero's tears at Sunderland could prove the lasting image of Tottenham's season if it ends with relegation, writes Phil McNulty.
